# AVC (Asia Vital Components) — Thermal Components for NVL72
Last updated: 2026-04-01

## Company Overview
- **HQ:** Taipei, Taiwan
- Listed on Taiwan Stock Exchange (6133.TW)
- Revenue: ~$300-400M USD annually
- Specialty: fans, heat sinks, vapor chambers, thermal modules for servers and networking
- Major OEM/ODM supplier to Dell, HP, Supermicro, Quanta, Foxconn

## Role in NVL72 Supply Chain
- AVC supplies **thermal components inside NVL72 compute trays**
- Specific products: fans (air cooling assist), vapor chambers, heat spreaders, and potentially manifold sub-assemblies
- GB300 NVL72 liquid cooling: AVC likely supplies internal thermal interface components and possibly manifold routing structures within the tray
- Long-standing relationship with server ODMs means AVC components are present in virtually all major server platforms

## Products Relevant to NVL72
- High-performance server fans (axial, centrifugal)
- Vapor chambers (heat spreading layer between GPU die and cold plate)
- Copper/aluminum heat sinks
- Thermal modules (integrated fan + heat exchanger assemblies)
- Potentially: manifold bracket assemblies, flow distribution components

## ADC Relevance
- AVC = inside the tray, at the thermal interface layer
- If GB300 NVL72 has supply constraints at the fan or vapor chamber level, AVC is the choke point
- Not a direct ADC vendor — supply chain intelligence

## URLs
- avc.com.tw

## Confidence: HIGH (AVC's thermal component role in server ODM supply is well documented; NVL72 specific role inferred from position)
